ayahs.textArabic: ٱقۡتَرَبَ لِلنَّاسِ حِسَابُهُمۡ وَهُمۡ فِي غَفۡلَةٖ مُّعۡرِضُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Iqtaraba lilnnasi hisabuhum wahum fee ghaflatin muAAridoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [The time of] their account has approached for the people, while they are in heedlessness turning away.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹The time of˺ people’s judgment has drawn near, yet they are heedlessly turning away.

ayahs.textArabic: مَا يَأۡتِيهِم مِّن ذِكۡرٖ مِّن رَّبِّهِم مُّحۡدَثٍ إِلَّا ٱسۡتَمَعُوهُ وَهُمۡ يَلۡعَبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma yateehim min thikrin min rabbihim muhdathin illa istamaAAoohu wahum yalAAaboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No mention comes to them anew from their Lord except that they listen to it while they are at play
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Whatever new reminder comes to them from their Lord, they only listen to it jokingly,

ayahs.textArabic: لَاهِيَةٗ قُلُوبُهُمۡۗ وَأَسَرُّواْ ٱلنَّجۡوَى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُواْ هَلۡ هَٰذَآ إِلَّا بَشَرٞ مِّثۡلُكُمۡۖ أَفَتَأۡتُونَ ٱلسِّحۡرَ وَأَنتُمۡ تُبۡصِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Lahiyatan quloobuhum waasarroo alnnajwa allatheena thalamoo hal hatha illa basharun mithlukum afatatoona alssihra waantum tubsiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: With their hearts distracted. And those who do wrong conceal their private conversation, [saying], "Is this [Prophet] except a human being like you? So would you approach magic while you are aware [of it]?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: with their hearts ˹totally˺ distracted. The evildoers would converse secretly, ˹saying,˺ “Is this ˹one˺ not human like yourselves? Would you fall for ˹this˺ witchcraft, even though you can ˹clearly˺ see?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ رَبِّي يَعۡلَمُ ٱلۡقَوۡلَ فِي ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِۖ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلۡعَلِيمُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbee yaAAlamu alqawla fee alssamai waalardi wahuwa alssameeAAu alAAaleemu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Prophet said, "My Lord knows whatever is said throughout the heaven and earth, and He is the Hearing, the Knowing."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The Prophet responded, “My Lord ˹fully˺ knows every word spoken in the heavens and the earth. For He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.”

ayahs.textArabic: بَلۡ قَالُوٓاْ أَضۡغَٰثُ أَحۡلَٰمِۭ بَلِ ٱفۡتَرَىٰهُ بَلۡ هُوَ شَاعِرٞ فَلۡيَأۡتِنَا بِـَٔايَةٖ كَمَآ أُرۡسِلَ ٱلۡأَوَّلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bal qaloo adghathu ahlamin bali iftarahu bal huwa shaAAirun falyatina biayatin kama orsila alawwaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they say, "[The revelation is but] a mixture of false dreams; rather, he has invented it; rather, he is a poet. So let him bring us a sign just as the previous [messengers] were sent [with miracles]."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yet they say, “This ˹Quran˺ is a set of confused dreams! No, he has fabricated it! No, he must be a poet! So let him bring us a ˹tangible˺ sign like those ˹prophets˺ sent before.”

ayahs.textArabic: مَآ ءَامَنَتۡ قَبۡلَهُم مِّن قَرۡيَةٍ أَهۡلَكۡنَٰهَآۖ أَفَهُمۡ يُؤۡمِنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ma amanat qablahum min qaryatin ahlaknaha afahum yuminoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Not a [single] city which We destroyed believed before them, so will they believe?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Not a ˹single˺ society We destroyed before them ever believed ˹after receiving the signs˺. Will these ˹pagans˺ then believe?

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا قَبۡلَكَ إِلَّا رِجَالٗا نُّوحِيٓ إِلَيۡهِمۡۖ فَسۡـَٔلُوٓاْ أَهۡلَ ٱلذِّكۡرِ إِن كُنتُمۡ لَا تَعۡلَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama arsalna qablaka illa rijalan noohee ilayhim faisaloo ahla alththikri in kuntum la taAAlamoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We sent not before you, [O Muhammad], except men to whom We revealed [the message], so ask the people of the message if you do not know.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We did not send ˹messengers˺ before you ˹O Prophet˺ except mere men inspired by Us. If you ˹polytheists˺ do not know ˹this already˺, then ask those who have knowledge ˹of the Scriptures˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا جَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ جَسَدٗا لَّا يَأۡكُلُونَ ٱلطَّعَامَ وَمَا كَانُواْ خَٰلِدِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama jaAAalnahum jasadan la yakuloona alttaAAama wama kanoo khalideena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We did not make the prophets forms not eating food, nor were they immortal [on earth].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We did not give those messengers ˹supernatural˺ bodies that did not need food, nor were they immortal.

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ صَدَقۡنَٰهُمُ ٱلۡوَعۡدَ فَأَنجَيۡنَٰهُمۡ وَمَن نَّشَآءُ وَأَهۡلَكۡنَا ٱلۡمُسۡرِفِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma sadaqnahumu alwaAAda faanjaynahum waman nashao waahlakna almusrifeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We fulfilled for them the promise, and We saved them and whom We willed and destroyed the transgressors.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We fulfilled Our promise to them, saving them along with whoever We willed and destroying the transgressors.

ayahs.textArabic: لَقَدۡ أَنزَلۡنَآ إِلَيۡكُمۡ كِتَٰبٗا فِيهِ ذِكۡرُكُمۡۚ أَفَلَا تَعۡقِلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Laqad anzalna ilaykum kitaban feehi thikrukum afala taAAqiloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We have certainly sent down to you a Book in which is your mention. Then will you not reason?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have surely revealed to you a Book, in which there is glory for you. Will you not then understand?

ayahs.textArabic: وَكَمۡ قَصَمۡنَا مِن قَرۡيَةٖ كَانَتۡ ظَالِمَةٗ وَأَنشَأۡنَا بَعۡدَهَا قَوۡمًا ءَاخَرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakam qasamna min qaryatin kanat thalimatan waanshana baAAdaha qawman akhareena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And how many a city which was unjust have We shattered and produced after it another people.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Imagine˺ how many societies of wrongdoers We have destroyed, raising up other people after them!

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَمَّآ أَحَسُّواْ بَأۡسَنَآ إِذَا هُم مِّنۡهَا يَرۡكُضُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falamma ahassoo basana itha hum minha yarkudoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when its inhabitants perceived Our punishment, at once they fled from it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When the wrongdoers sensed ˹the arrival of˺ Our torment, they started to run away from their cities.

ayahs.textArabic: لَا تَرۡكُضُواْ وَٱرۡجِعُوٓاْ إِلَىٰ مَآ أُتۡرِفۡتُمۡ فِيهِ وَمَسَٰكِنِكُمۡ لَعَلَّكُمۡ تُسۡـَٔلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La tarkudoo wairjiAAoo ila ma otriftum feehi wamasakinikum laAAallakum tusaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Some angels said], "Do not flee but return to where you were given luxury and to your homes - perhaps you will be questioned."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They were told,˺ “Do not run away! Return to your luxuries and your homes, so you may be questioned ˹about your fate˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ يَٰوَيۡلَنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo ya waylana inna kunna thalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "O woe to us! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They cried, “Woe to us! We have surely been wrongdoers.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَمَا زَالَت تِّلۡكَ دَعۡوَىٰهُمۡ حَتَّىٰ جَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ حَصِيدًا خَٰمِدِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fama zalat tilka daAAwahum hatta jaAAalnahum haseedan khamideena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that declaration of theirs did not cease until We made them [as] a harvest [mowed down], extinguished [like a fire].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They kept repeating their cry until We mowed them down, ˹leaving them˺ lifeless.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا خَلَقۡنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا لَٰعِبِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama khalaqna alssamaa waalarda wama baynahuma laAAibeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We did not create the heaven and earth and that between them in play.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We did not create the heavens and the earth and everything in between for sport.

ayahs.textArabic: لَوۡ أَرَدۡنَآ أَن نَّتَّخِذَ لَهۡوٗا لَّٱتَّخَذۡنَٰهُ مِن لَّدُنَّآ إِن كُنَّا فَٰعِلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Law aradna an nattakhitha lahwan laittakhathnahu min ladunna in kunna faAAileena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Had We intended to take a diversion, We could have taken it from [what is] with Us - if [indeed] We were to do so.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had We intended to take ˹some˺ amusement, We could have found it in Our presence, if that had been Our Will.

ayahs.textArabic: بَلۡ نَقۡذِفُ بِٱلۡحَقِّ عَلَى ٱلۡبَٰطِلِ فَيَدۡمَغُهُۥ فَإِذَا هُوَ زَاهِقٞۚ وَلَكُمُ ٱلۡوَيۡلُ مِمَّا تَصِفُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bal naqthifu bialhaqqi AAala albatili fayadmaghuhu faitha huwa zahiqun walakumu alwaylu mimma tasifoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Rather, We dash the truth upon falsehood, and it destroys it, and thereupon it departs. And for you is destruction from that which you describe.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, We hurl the truth against falsehood, leaving it crushed, and it quickly vanishes. And woe be to you for what you claim!

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَهُۥ مَن فِي ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِۚ وَمَنۡ عِندَهُۥ لَا يَسۡتَكۡبِرُونَ عَنۡ عِبَادَتِهِۦ وَلَا يَسۡتَحۡسِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walahu man fee alssamawati waalardi waman AAindahu la yastakbiroona AAan AAibadatihi wala yastahsiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: To Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. And those near Him are not prevented by arrogance from His worship, nor do they tire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: To Him belong all those in the heavens and the earth. And those nearest to Him are not too proud to worship Him, nor do they tire.

ayahs.textArabic: يُسَبِّحُونَ ٱلَّيۡلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ لَا يَفۡتُرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yusabbihoona allayla waalnnahara la yafturoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They exalt [Him] night and day [and] do not slacken.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They glorify ˹Him˺ day and night, never wavering.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمِ ٱتَّخَذُوٓاْ ءَالِهَةٗ مِّنَ ٱلۡأَرۡضِ هُمۡ يُنشِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ami ittakhathoo alihatan mina alardi hum yunshiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or have men taken for themselves gods from the earth who resurrect [the dead]?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or have they taken gods from the earth, who can raise the dead?

ayahs.textArabic: لَوۡ كَانَ فِيهِمَآ ءَالِهَةٌ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ لَفَسَدَتَاۚ فَسُبۡحَٰنَ ٱللَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَرۡشِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Law kana feehima alihatun illa Allahu lafasadata fasubhana Allahi rabbi alAAarshi AAamma yasifoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Had there been within the heavens and earth gods besides Allah, they both would have been ruined. So exalted is Allah, Lord of the Throne, above what they describe.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had there been other gods besides Allah in the heavens or the earth, both ˹realms˺ would have surely been corrupted. So Glorified is Allah, Lord of the Throne, far above what they claim.

ayahs.textArabic: لَا يُسۡـَٔلُ عَمَّا يَفۡعَلُ وَهُمۡ يُسۡـَٔلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La yusalu AAamma yafAAalu wahum yusaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He is not questioned about what He does, but they will be questioned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He cannot be questioned about what He does, but they will ˹all˺ be questioned.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمِ ٱتَّخَذُواْ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ ءَالِهَةٗۖ قُلۡ هَاتُواْ بُرۡهَٰنَكُمۡۖ هَٰذَا ذِكۡرُ مَن مَّعِيَ وَذِكۡرُ مَن قَبۡلِيۚ بَلۡ أَكۡثَرُهُمۡ لَا يَعۡلَمُونَ ٱلۡحَقَّۖ فَهُم مُّعۡرِضُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ami ittakhathoo min doonihi alihatan qul hatoo burhanakum hatha thikru man maAAiya wathikru man qablee bal aktharuhum la yaAAlamoona alhaqqa fahum muAAridoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or have they taken gods besides Him? Say, [O Muhammad], "Produce your proof. This [Qur'an] is the message for those with me and the message of those before me." But most of them do not know the truth, so they are turning away.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or have they taken other gods besides Him? Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Show ˹me˺ your proof. Here is ˹the Quran,˺ the Reminder for those with me; along with ˹earlier Scriptures,˺ the Reminder for those before me.” But most of them do not know the truth, so they turn away.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا مِن قَبۡلِكَ مِن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا نُوحِيٓ إِلَيۡهِ أَنَّهُۥ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنَا۠ فَٱعۡبُدُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama arsalna min qablika min rasoolin illa noohee ilayhi annahu la ilaha illa ana faoAAbudooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We sent not before you any messenger except that We revealed to him that, "There is no deity except Me, so worship Me."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We never sent a messenger before you ˹O Prophet˺ without revealing to him: “There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Me, so worship Me ˹alone˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالُواْ ٱتَّخَذَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ وَلَدٗاۗ سُبۡحَٰنَهُۥۚ بَلۡ عِبَادٞ مُّكۡرَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo ittakhatha alrrahmanu waladan subhanahu bal AAibadun mukramoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they say, "The Most Merciful has taken a son." Exalted is He! Rather, they are [but] honored servants.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And they say, “The Most Compassionate has offspring!” Glory be to Him! In fact, those ˹angels˺ are only ˹His˺ honoured servants,

ayahs.textArabic: لَا يَسۡبِقُونَهُۥ بِٱلۡقَوۡلِ وَهُم بِأَمۡرِهِۦ يَعۡمَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La yasbiqoonahu bialqawli wahum biamrihi yaAAmaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They cannot precede Him in word, and they act by His command.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: who do not speak until He has spoken, ˹only˺ acting at His command.

ayahs.textArabic: يَعۡلَمُ مَا بَيۡنَ أَيۡدِيهِمۡ وَمَا خَلۡفَهُمۡ وَلَا يَشۡفَعُونَ إِلَّا لِمَنِ ٱرۡتَضَىٰ وَهُم مِّنۡ خَشۡيَتِهِۦ مُشۡفِقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: YaAAlamu ma bayna aydeehim wama khalfahum wala yashfaAAoona illa limani irtada wahum min khashyatihi mushfiqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, and they cannot intercede except on behalf of one whom He approves. And they, from fear of Him, are apprehensive.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He ˹fully˺ knows what is ahead of them and what is behind them. They do not intercede except for whom He approves, and they tremble in awe of Him.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَمَن يَقُلۡ مِنۡهُمۡ إِنِّيٓ إِلَٰهٞ مِّن دُونِهِۦ فَذَٰلِكَ نَجۡزِيهِ جَهَنَّمَۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجۡزِي ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waman yaqul minhum innee ilahun min doonihi fathalika najzeehi jahannama kathalika najzee alththalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And whoever of them should say, "Indeed, I am a god besides Him"- that one We would recompense with Hell. Thus do We recompense the wrongdoers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Whoever of them were to say, “I am a god besides Him,” they would be rewarded with Hell by Us. This is how We reward the wrongdoers.

ayahs.textArabic: أَوَلَمۡ يَرَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓاْ أَنَّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ كَانَتَا رَتۡقٗا فَفَتَقۡنَٰهُمَاۖ وَجَعَلۡنَا مِنَ ٱلۡمَآءِ كُلَّ شَيۡءٍ حَيٍّۚ أَفَلَا يُؤۡمِنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Awalam yara allatheena kafaroo anna alssamawati waalarda kanata ratqan fafataqnahuma wajaAAalna mina almai kulla shayin hayyin afala yuminoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Have those who disbelieved not considered that the heavens and the earth were a joined entity, and We separated them and made from water every living thing? Then will they not believe?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do the disbelievers not realize that the heavens and earth were ˹once˺ one mass then We split them apart? And We created from water every living thing. Will they not then believe?

ayahs.textArabic: وَجَعَلۡنَا فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ رَوَٰسِيَ أَن تَمِيدَ بِهِمۡ وَجَعَلۡنَا فِيهَا فِجَاجٗا سُبُلٗا لَّعَلَّهُمۡ يَهۡتَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WajaAAalna fee alardi rawasiya an tameeda bihim wajaAAalna feeha fijajan subulan laAAallahum yahtadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We placed within the earth firmly set mountains, lest it should shift with them, and We made therein [mountain] passes [as] roads that they might be guided.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We have placed firm mountains upon the earth so it does not shake with them, and made in it broad pathways so they may find their way.

ayahs.textArabic: وَجَعَلۡنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ سَقۡفٗا مَّحۡفُوظٗاۖ وَهُمۡ عَنۡ ءَايَٰتِهَا مُعۡرِضُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WajaAAalna alssamaa saqfan mahfoothan wahum AAan ayatiha muAAridoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We made the sky a protected ceiling, but they, from its signs, are turning away.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We have made the sky a well-protected canopy, still they turn away from its signs.

ayahs.textArabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي خَلَقَ ٱلَّيۡلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ وَٱلشَّمۡسَ وَٱلۡقَمَرَۖ كُلّٞ فِي فَلَكٖ يَسۡبَحُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahuwa allathee khalaqa allayla waalnnahara waalshshamsa waalqamara kullun fee falakin yasbahoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is He who created the night and the day and the sun and the moon; all [heavenly bodies] in an orbit are swimming.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He is the One Who created the day and the night, the sun and the moon—each travelling in an orbit.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا جَعَلۡنَا لِبَشَرٖ مِّن قَبۡلِكَ ٱلۡخُلۡدَۖ أَفَإِيْن مِّتَّ فَهُمُ ٱلۡخَٰلِدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama jaAAalna libasharin min qablika alkhulda afain mitta fahumu alkhalidoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We did not grant to any man before you eternity [on earth]; so if you die - would they be eternal?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have not granted immortality to any human before you ˹O Prophet˺: so if you die, will they live forever?

ayahs.textArabic: كُلُّ نَفۡسٖ ذَآئِقَةُ ٱلۡمَوۡتِۗ وَنَبۡلُوكُم بِٱلشَّرِّ وَٱلۡخَيۡرِ فِتۡنَةٗۖ وَإِلَيۡنَا تُرۡجَعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kullu nafsin thaiqatu almawti wanablookum bialshsharri waalkhayri fitnatan wailayna turjaAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Every soul will taste death. And We test you with evil and with good as trial; and to Us you will be returned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Every soul will taste death. And We test you ˹O humanity˺ with good and evil as a trial, then to Us you will ˹all˺ be returned.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِذَا رَءَاكَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓاْ إِن يَتَّخِذُونَكَ إِلَّا هُزُوًا أَهَٰذَا ٱلَّذِي يَذۡكُرُ ءَالِهَتَكُمۡ وَهُم بِذِكۡرِ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ هُمۡ كَٰفِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waitha raaka allatheena kafaroo in yattakhithoonaka illa huzuwan ahatha allathee yathkuru alihatakum wahum bithikri alrrahmani hum kafiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when those who disbelieve see you, [O Muhammad], they take you not except in ridicule, [saying], "Is this the one who insults your gods?" And they are, at the mention of the Most Merciful, disbelievers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When the disbelievers see you ˹O Prophet˺, they only make fun of you, ˹saying,˺ “Is this the one who speaks ˹ill˺ of your gods?” while they disbelieve at the mention of the Most Compassionate.

ayahs.textArabic: خُلِقَ ٱلۡإِنسَٰنُ مِنۡ عَجَلٖۚ سَأُوْرِيكُمۡ ءَايَٰتِي فَلَا تَسۡتَعۡجِلُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Khuliqa alinsanu min AAajalin saoreekum ayatee fala tastaAAjiloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Man was created of haste. I will show you My signs, so do not impatiently urge Me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Humankind is made of haste. I will soon show you My signs, so do not ask Me to hasten them.

ayahs.textArabic: وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَٰذَا ٱلۡوَعۡدُ إِن كُنتُمۡ صَٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wayaqooloona mata hatha alwaAAdu in kuntum sadiqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they say, "When is this promise, if you should be truthful?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They ask ˹the believers˺, “When will this threat come to pass if what you say is true?”

ayahs.textArabic: لَوۡ يَعۡلَمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ حِينَ لَا يَكُفُّونَ عَن وُجُوهِهِمُ ٱلنَّارَ وَلَا عَن ظُهُورِهِمۡ وَلَا هُمۡ يُنصَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Law yaAAlamu allatheena kafaroo heena la yakuffoona AAan wujoohihimu alnnara wala AAan thuhoorihim wala hum yunsaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: If those who disbelieved but knew the time when they will not avert the Fire from their faces or from their backs and they will not be aided...
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: If only the disbelievers knew that a time will come when they will not be able to keep the Fire off their faces or backs, nor will they be helped.

ayahs.textArabic: بَلۡ تَأۡتِيهِم بَغۡتَةٗ فَتَبۡهَتُهُمۡ فَلَا يَسۡتَطِيعُونَ رَدَّهَا وَلَا هُمۡ يُنظَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bal tateehim baghtatan fatabhatuhum fala yastateeAAoona raddaha wala hum yuntharoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Rather, it will come to them unexpectedly and bewilder them, and they will not be able to repel it, nor will they be reprieved.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, the Hour will take them by surprise, leaving them stunned. So they will not be able to avert it, nor will it be delayed from them.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدِ ٱسۡتُهۡزِئَ بِرُسُلٖ مِّن قَبۡلِكَ فَحَاقَ بِٱلَّذِينَ سَخِرُواْ مِنۡهُم مَّا كَانُواْ بِهِۦ يَسۡتَهۡزِءُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqadi istuhzia birusulin min qablika fahaqa biallatheena sakhiroo minhum ma kanoo bihi yastahzioona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And already were messengers ridiculed before you, but those who mocked them were enveloped by what they used to ridicule.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Other˺ messengers had already been ridiculed before you ˹O Prophet˺, but those who mocked them were overtaken by what they used to ridicule.

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ مَن يَكۡلَؤُكُم بِٱلَّيۡلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ مِنَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِۚ بَلۡ هُمۡ عَن ذِكۡرِ رَبِّهِم مُّعۡرِضُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul man yaklaokum biallayli waalnnahari mina alrrahmani bal hum AAan thikri rabbihim muAAridoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "Who can protect you at night or by day from the Most Merciful?" But they are, from the remembrance of their Lord, turning away.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ask ˹them, O Prophet,˺ “Who can defend you by day or by night against the Most Compassionate?” Still they turn away from the remembrance of their Lord.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ لَهُمۡ ءَالِهَةٞ تَمۡنَعُهُم مِّن دُونِنَاۚ لَا يَسۡتَطِيعُونَ نَصۡرَ أَنفُسِهِمۡ وَلَا هُم مِّنَّا يُصۡحَبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am lahum alihatun tamnaAAuhum min doonina la yastateeAAoona nasra anfusihim wala hum minna yushaboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do they have gods to defend them other than Us? They are unable [even] to help themselves, nor can they be protected from Us.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do they have gods—other than Us—that can protect them? They cannot ˹even˺ protect themselves, nor will they be aided against Us.

ayahs.textArabic: بَلۡ مَتَّعۡنَا هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ وَءَابَآءَهُمۡ حَتَّىٰ طَالَ عَلَيۡهِمُ ٱلۡعُمُرُۗ أَفَلَا يَرَوۡنَ أَنَّا نَأۡتِي ٱلۡأَرۡضَ نَنقُصُهَا مِنۡ أَطۡرَافِهَآۚ أَفَهُمُ ٱلۡغَٰلِبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bal mattaAAna haolai waabaahum hatta tala AAalayhimu alAAumuru afala yarawna anna natee alarda nanqusuha min atrafiha afahumu alghaliboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But, [on the contrary], We have provided good things for these [disbelievers] and their fathers until life was prolonged for them. Then do they not see that We set upon the land, reducing it from its borders? So it is they who will overcome?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, We have allowed enjoyment for these ˹Meccans˺ and their forefathers for such a long time ˹that they took it for granted˺. Do they not see that We gradually reduce ˹their˺ land from its borders? Is it they who will then prevail?

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ إِنَّمَآ أُنذِرُكُم بِٱلۡوَحۡيِۚ وَلَا يَسۡمَعُ ٱلصُّمُّ ٱلدُّعَآءَ إِذَا مَا يُنذَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul innama onthirukum bialwahyi wala yasmaAAu alssummu aldduAAaa itha ma yuntharoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "I only warn you by revelation." But the deaf do not hear the call when they are warned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “I warn you only by revelation.” But the deaf cannot hear the call when they are warned!

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَئِن مَّسَّتۡهُمۡ نَفۡحَةٞ مِّنۡ عَذَابِ رَبِّكَ لَيَقُولُنَّ يَٰوَيۡلَنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walain massathum nafhatun min AAathabi rabbika layaqoolunna ya waylana inna kunna thalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if [as much as] a whiff of the punishment of your Lord should touch them, they would surely say, "O woe to us! Indeed, we have been wrongdoers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: If they were touched by even a breath of your Lord’s torment, they would certainly cry, “Woe to us! We have really been wrongdoers.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَضَعُ ٱلۡمَوَٰزِينَ ٱلۡقِسۡطَ لِيَوۡمِ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ فَلَا تُظۡلَمُ نَفۡسٞ شَيۡـٔٗاۖ وَإِن كَانَ مِثۡقَالَ حَبَّةٖ مِّنۡ خَرۡدَلٍ أَتَيۡنَا بِهَاۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِنَا حَٰسِبِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WanadaAAu almawazeena alqista liyawmi alqiyamati fala tuthlamu nafsun shayan wain kana mithqala habbatin min khardalin atayna biha wakafa bina hasibeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We place the scales of justice for the Day of Resurrection, so no soul will be treated unjustly at all. And if there is [even] the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it forth. And sufficient are We as accountant.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We will set up the scales of justice on the Day of Judgment, so no soul will be wronged in the least. And ˹even˺ if a deed is the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it forth. And sufficient are We as a ˹vigilant˺ Reckoner.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَا مُوسَىٰ وَهَٰرُونَ ٱلۡفُرۡقَانَ وَضِيَآءٗ وَذِكۡرٗا لِّلۡمُتَّقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad atayna moosa waharoona alfurqana wadiyaan wathikran lilmuttaqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We had already given Moses and Aaron the criterion and a light and a reminder for the righteous
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We granted Moses and Aaron the standard ˹to distinguish between right and wrong˺—a light and a reminder for the righteous,

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يَخۡشَوۡنَ رَبَّهُم بِٱلۡغَيۡبِ وَهُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّاعَةِ مُشۡفِقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allatheena yakhshawna rabbahum bialghaybi wahum mina alssaAAati mushfiqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Who fear their Lord unseen, while they are of the Hour apprehensive.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: who are in awe of their Lord without seeing Him, and are fearful of the Hour.

ayahs.textArabic: وَهَٰذَا ذِكۡرٞ مُّبَارَكٌ أَنزَلۡنَٰهُۚ أَفَأَنتُمۡ لَهُۥ مُنكِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahatha thikrun mubarakun anzalnahu afaantum lahu munkiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And this [Qur'an] is a blessed message which We have sent down. Then are you with it unacquainted?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And this ˹Quran˺ is a blessed reminder which We have revealed. Will you ˹pagans˺ then deny it?

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَآ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ رُشۡدَهُۥ مِن قَبۡلُ وَكُنَّا بِهِۦ عَٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad atayna ibraheema rushdahu min qablu wakunna bihi AAalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We had certainly given Abraham his sound judgement before, and We were of him well-Knowing
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And indeed, We had granted Abraham sound judgment early on, for We knew him well ˹to be worthy of it˺.

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوۡمِهِۦ مَا هَٰذِهِ ٱلتَّمَاثِيلُ ٱلَّتِيٓ أَنتُمۡ لَهَا عَٰكِفُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith qala liabeehi waqawmihi ma hathihi alttamatheelu allatee antum laha AAakifoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When he said to his father and his people, "What are these statues to which you are devoted?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Remember˺ when he questioned his father and his people, “What are these statues to which you are so devoted?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ وَجَدۡنَآ ءَابَآءَنَا لَهَا عَٰبِدِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo wajadna abaana laha AAabideena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "We found our fathers worshippers of them."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They replied, “We found our forefathers worshipping them.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ لَقَدۡ كُنتُمۡ أَنتُمۡ وَءَابَآؤُكُمۡ فِي ضَلَٰلٖ مُّبِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala laqad kuntum antum waabaokum fee dalalin mubeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "You were certainly, you and your fathers, in manifest error."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He responded, “Indeed, you and your forefathers have been clearly astray.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُوٓاْ أَجِئۡتَنَا بِٱلۡحَقِّ أَمۡ أَنتَ مِنَ ٱللَّٰعِبِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo ajitana bialhaqqi am anta mina allaAAibeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Have you come to us with truth, or are you of those who jest?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They asked, “Have you come to us with the truth, or is this a joke?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ بَل رَّبُّكُمۡ رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ ٱلَّذِي فَطَرَهُنَّ وَأَنَا۠ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكُم مِّنَ ٱلشَّٰهِدِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala bal rabbukum rabbu alssamawati waalardi allathee fatarahunna waana AAala thalikum mina alshshahideena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "[No], rather, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth who created them, and I, to that, am of those who testify.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He replied, “In fact, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who created them ˹both˺. And to that I bear witness.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَتَٱللَّهِ لَأَكِيدَنَّ أَصۡنَٰمَكُم بَعۡدَ أَن تُوَلُّواْ مُدۡبِرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WataAllahi laakeedanna asnamakum baAAda an tuwalloo mudbireena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [I swear] by Allah, I will surely plan against your idols after you have turned and gone away."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Then he said to himself,˺ “By Allah! I will surely plot against your idols after you have turned your backs and gone away.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَجَعَلَهُمۡ جُذَٰذًا إِلَّا كَبِيرٗا لَّهُمۡ لَعَلَّهُمۡ إِلَيۡهِ يَرۡجِعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: FajaAAalahum juthathan illa kabeeran lahum laAAallahum ilayhi yarjiAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So he made them into fragments, except a large one among them, that they might return to it [and question].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So he smashed them into pieces, except the biggest of them, so they might turn to it ˹for answers˺.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ مَن فَعَلَ هَٰذَا بِـَٔالِهَتِنَآ إِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo man faAAala hatha bialihatina innahu lamina alththalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Who has done this to our gods? Indeed, he is of the wrongdoers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They protested, “Who dared do this to our gods? It must be an evildoer!”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ سَمِعۡنَا فَتٗى يَذۡكُرُهُمۡ يُقَالُ لَهُۥٓ إِبۡرَٰهِيمُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo samiAAna fatan yathkuruhum yuqalu lahu ibraheemu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "We heard a young man mention them who is called Abraham."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Some said, “We heard a young man, called Abraham, speaking ˹ill˺ of them.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ فَأۡتُواْ بِهِۦ عَلَىٰٓ أَعۡيُنِ ٱلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَشۡهَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo fatoo bihi AAala aAAyuni alnnasi laAAallahum yashhadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Then bring him before the eyes of the people that they may testify."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They demanded, “Bring him before the eyes of the people, so that they may witness ˹his trial˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُوٓاْ ءَأَنتَ فَعَلۡتَ هَٰذَا بِـَٔالِهَتِنَا يَٰٓإِبۡرَٰهِيمُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo aanta faAAalta hatha bialihatina ya ibraheemu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Have you done this to our gods, O Abraham?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They asked, “Was it you who did this to our gods, O Abraham?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ بَلۡ فَعَلَهُۥ كَبِيرُهُمۡ هَٰذَا فَسۡـَٔلُوهُمۡ إِن كَانُواْ يَنطِقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala bal faAAalahu kabeeruhum hatha faisaloohum in kanoo yantiqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "Rather, this - the largest of them - did it, so ask them, if they should [be able to] speak."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He replied ˹sarcastically˺, “No, this one—the biggest of them—did it! So ask them, if they can talk!”

ayahs.textArabic: فَرَجَعُوٓاْ إِلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمۡ فَقَالُوٓاْ إِنَّكُمۡ أَنتُمُ ٱلظَّٰلِمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: FarajaAAoo ila anfusihim faqaloo innakum antumu alththalimoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So they returned to [blaming] themselves and said [to each other], "Indeed, you are the wrongdoers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So they came back to their senses, saying ˹to one another˺, “You yourselves are truly the wrongdoers!”

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ نُكِسُواْ عَلَىٰ رُءُوسِهِمۡ لَقَدۡ عَلِمۡتَ مَا هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ يَنطِقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma nukisoo AAala ruoosihim laqad AAalimta ma haolai yantiqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then they reversed themselves, [saying], "You have already known that these do not speak!"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then they ˹quickly˺ regressed to their ˹original˺ mind-set, ˹arguing,˺ “You already know that those ˹idols˺ cannot talk.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ أَفَتَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُكُمۡ شَيۡـٔٗا وَلَا يَضُرُّكُمۡ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala afataAAbudoona min dooni Allahi ma la yanfaAAukum shayan wala yadurrukum
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "Then do you worship instead of Allah that which does not benefit you at all or harm you?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He rebuked ˹them˺, “Do you then worship—instead of Allah—what can neither benefit nor harm you in any way?

ayahs.textArabic: أُفّٖ لَّكُمۡ وَلِمَا تَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِۚ أَفَلَا تَعۡقِلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Offin lakum walima taAAbudoona min dooni Allahi afala taAAqiloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Uff to you and to what you worship instead of Allah. Then will you not use reason?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Shame on you and whatever you worship instead of Allah! Do you not have any sense?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ حَرِّقُوهُ وَٱنصُرُوٓاْ ءَالِهَتَكُمۡ إِن كُنتُمۡ فَٰعِلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo harriqoohu waonsuroo alihatakum in kuntum faAAileena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Burn him and support your gods - if you are to act."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They concluded, “Burn him up to avenge your gods, if you must act.”

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡنَا يَٰنَارُ كُونِي بَرۡدٗا وَسَلَٰمًا عَلَىٰٓ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qulna ya naru koonee bardan wasalaman AAala ibraheema
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Allah said, "O fire, be coolness and safety upon Abraham."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We ordered, “O fire! Be cool and safe for Abraham!”

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَرَادُواْ بِهِۦ كَيۡدٗا فَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمُ ٱلۡأَخۡسَرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waaradoo bihi kaydan fajaAAalnahumu alakhsareena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they intended for him harm, but We made them the greatest losers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They had sought to harm him, but We made them the worst losers.

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَجَّيۡنَٰهُ وَلُوطًا إِلَى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ ٱلَّتِي بَٰرَكۡنَا فِيهَا لِلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wanajjaynahu walootan ila alardi allatee barakna feeha lilAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We delivered him and Lot to the land which We had blessed for the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We delivered him, along with Lot, to the land We had showered with blessings for all people.

ayahs.textArabic: وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥٓ إِسۡحَٰقَ وَيَعۡقُوبَ نَافِلَةٗۖ وَكُلّٗا جَعَلۡنَا صَٰلِحِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wawahabna lahu ishaqa wayaAAqooba nafilatan wakullan jaAAalna saliheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob in addition, and all [of them] We made righteous.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We blessed him with Isaac ˹as a son˺ and Jacob ˹as a grandson˺, as an additional favour—making all of them righteous.

ayahs.textArabic: وَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ أَئِمَّةٗ يَهۡدُونَ بِأَمۡرِنَا وَأَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَيۡهِمۡ فِعۡلَ ٱلۡخَيۡرَٰتِ وَإِقَامَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَإِيتَآءَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةِۖ وَكَانُواْ لَنَا عَٰبِدِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WajaAAalnahum aimmatan yahdoona biamrina waawhayna ilayhim fiAAla alkhayrati waiqama alssalati waeetaa alzzakati wakanoo lana AAabideena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We made them leaders guiding by Our command. And We inspired to them the doing of good deeds, establishment of prayer, and giving of zakah; and they were worshippers of Us.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We ˹also˺ made them leaders, guiding by Our command, and inspired them to do good deeds, establish prayer, and pay alms-tax. And they were devoted to Our worship.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلُوطًا ءَاتَيۡنَٰهُ حُكۡمٗا وَعِلۡمٗا وَنَجَّيۡنَٰهُ مِنَ ٱلۡقَرۡيَةِ ٱلَّتِي كَانَت تَّعۡمَلُ ٱلۡخَبَٰٓئِثَۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ قَوۡمَ سَوۡءٖ فَٰسِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walootan ataynahu hukman waAAilman wanajjaynahu mina alqaryati allatee kanat taAAmalu alkhabaitha innahum kanoo qawma sawin fasiqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And to Lot We gave judgement and knowledge, and We saved him from the city that was committing wicked deeds. Indeed, they were a people of evil, defiantly disobedient.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And to Lot We gave wisdom and knowledge, and delivered him from the society engrossed in shameful practices. They were certainly an evil, rebellious people.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَدۡخَلۡنَٰهُ فِي رَحۡمَتِنَآۖ إِنَّهُۥ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰلِحِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waadkhalnahu fee rahmatina innahu mina alssaliheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We admitted him into Our mercy. Indeed, he was of the righteous.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We admitted him into Our mercy, ˹for˺ he was truly one of the righteous.

ayahs.textArabic: وَنُوحًا إِذۡ نَادَىٰ مِن قَبۡلُ فَٱسۡتَجَبۡنَا لَهُۥ فَنَجَّيۡنَٰهُ وَأَهۡلَهُۥ مِنَ ٱلۡكَرۡبِ ٱلۡعَظِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wanoohan ith nada min qablu faistajabna lahu fanajjaynahu waahlahu mina alkarbi alAAatheemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [mention] Noah, when he called [to Allah] before [that time], so We responded to him and saved him and his family from the great flood.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹remember˺ when Noah had cried out to Us earlier, so We responded to him and delivered him and his family from the great distress.

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَصَرۡنَٰهُ مِنَ ٱلۡقَوۡمِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُواْ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَآۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ قَوۡمَ سَوۡءٖ فَأَغۡرَقۡنَٰهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wanasarnahu mina alqawmi allatheena kaththaboo biayatina innahum kanoo qawma sawin faaghraqnahum ajmaAAeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We saved him from the people who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a people of evil, so We drowned them, all together.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We made him prevail over those who had rejected Our signs. They were truly an evil people, so We drowned them all.

ayahs.textArabic: وَدَاوُۥدَ وَسُلَيۡمَٰنَ إِذۡ يَحۡكُمَانِ فِي ٱلۡحَرۡثِ إِذۡ نَفَشَتۡ فِيهِ غَنَمُ ٱلۡقَوۡمِ وَكُنَّا لِحُكۡمِهِمۡ شَٰهِدِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wadawooda wasulaymana ith yahkumani fee alharthi ith nafashat feehi ghanamu alqawmi wakunna lihukmihim shahideena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [mention] David and Solomon, when they judged concerning the field - when the sheep of a people overran it [at night], and We were witness to their judgement.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹remember˺ when David and Solomon passed judgment regarding the crops ruined ˹at night˺ by someone’s sheep, and We were witness to their judgments.

ayahs.textArabic: فَفَهَّمۡنَٰهَا سُلَيۡمَٰنَۚ وَكُلًّا ءَاتَيۡنَا حُكۡمٗا وَعِلۡمٗاۚ وَسَخَّرۡنَا مَعَ دَاوُۥدَ ٱلۡجِبَالَ يُسَبِّحۡنَ وَٱلطَّيۡرَۚ وَكُنَّا فَٰعِلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fafahhamnaha sulaymana wakullan atayna hukman waAAilman wasakhkharna maAAa dawooda aljibala yusabbihna waalttayra wakunna faAAileena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We gave understanding of the case to Solomon, and to each [of them] We gave judgement and knowledge. And We subjected the mountains to exalt [Us], along with David and [also] the birds. And We were doing [that].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We guided ˹young˺ Solomon to a fairer settlement, and granted each of them wisdom and knowledge. We subjected the mountains as well as the birds to hymn ˹Our praises˺ along with David. It is We Who did ˹it all˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَعَلَّمۡنَٰهُ صَنۡعَةَ لَبُوسٖ لَّكُمۡ لِتُحۡصِنَكُم مِّنۢ بَأۡسِكُمۡۖ فَهَلۡ أَنتُمۡ شَٰكِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaAAallamnahu sanAAata laboosin lakum lituhsinakum min basikum fahal antum shakiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We taught him the fashioning of coats of armor to protect you from your [enemy in] battle. So will you then be grateful?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We taught him the art of making body armour to protect you in battle. Will you then be grateful?

ayahs.textArabic: وَلِسُلَيۡمَٰنَ ٱلرِّيحَ عَاصِفَةٗ تَجۡرِي بِأَمۡرِهِۦٓ إِلَى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ ٱلَّتِي بَٰرَكۡنَا فِيهَاۚ وَكُنَّا بِكُلِّ شَيۡءٍ عَٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walisulaymana alrreeha AAasifatan tajree biamrihi ila alardi allatee barakna feeha wakunna bikulli shayin AAalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And to Solomon [We subjected] the wind, blowing forcefully, proceeding by his command toward the land which We had blessed. And We are ever, of all things, Knowing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And to Solomon We subjected the raging winds, blowing by his command to the land We had showered with blessings. It is We Who know everything.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمِنَ ٱلشَّيَٰطِينِ مَن يَغُوصُونَ لَهُۥ وَيَعۡمَلُونَ عَمَلٗا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَۖ وَكُنَّا لَهُمۡ حَٰفِظِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wamina alshshayateeni man yaghoosoona lahu wayaAAmaloona AAamalan doona thalika wakunna lahum hafitheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And of the devils were those who dived for him and did work other than that. And We were of them a guardian.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹We subjected˺ some jinn that dived for him, and performed other duties. It is We Who kept them in check.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَأَيُّوبَ إِذۡ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنِّي مَسَّنِيَ ٱلضُّرُّ وَأَنتَ أَرۡحَمُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waayyooba ith nada rabbahu annee massaniya alddurru waanta arhamu alrrahimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [mention] Job, when he called to his Lord, "Indeed, adversity has touched me, and you are the Most Merciful of the merciful."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹remember˺ when Job cried out to his Lord, “I have been touched with adversity, and You are the Most Merciful of the merciful.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱسۡتَجَبۡنَا لَهُۥ فَكَشَفۡنَا مَا بِهِۦ مِن ضُرّٖۖ وَءَاتَيۡنَٰهُ أَهۡلَهُۥ وَمِثۡلَهُم مَّعَهُمۡ رَحۡمَةٗ مِّنۡ عِندِنَا وَذِكۡرَىٰ لِلۡعَٰبِدِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faistajabna lahu fakashafna ma bihi min durrin waataynahu ahlahu wamithlahum maAAahum rahmatan min AAindina wathikra lilAAabideena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We responded to him and removed what afflicted him of adversity. And We gave him [back] his family and the like thereof with them as mercy from Us and a reminder for the worshippers [of Allah].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We answered his prayer and removed his adversity, and gave him back his family, twice as many, as a mercy from Us and a lesson for the ˹devoted˺ worshippers.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِسۡمَٰعِيلَ وَإِدۡرِيسَ وَذَا ٱلۡكِفۡلِۖ كُلّٞ مِّنَ ٱلصَّٰبِرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaismaAAeela waidreesa watha alkifli kullun mina alssabireena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [mention] Ishmael and Idrees and Dhul-Kifl; all were of the patient.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹remember˺ Ishmael, Enoch, and Ⱬul-Kifl. They were all steadfast.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَدۡخَلۡنَٰهُمۡ فِي رَحۡمَتِنَآۖ إِنَّهُم مِّنَ ٱلصَّٰلِحِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waadkhalnahum fee rahmatina innahum mina alssaliheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We admitted them into Our mercy. Indeed, they were of the righteous.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We admitted them into Our mercy, for they were truly of the righteous.

ayahs.textArabic: وَذَا ٱلنُّونِ إِذ ذَّهَبَ مُغَٰضِبٗا فَظَنَّ أَن لَّن نَّقۡدِرَ عَلَيۡهِ فَنَادَىٰ فِي ٱلظُّلُمَٰتِ أَن لَّآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنتَ سُبۡحَٰنَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Watha alnnooni ith thahaba mughadiban fathanna an lan naqdira AAalayhi fanada fee alththulumati an la ilaha illa anta subhanaka innee kuntu mina alththalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [mention] the man of the fish, when he went off in anger and thought that We would not decree [anything] upon him. And he called out within the darknesses, "There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹remember˺ when the Man of the Whale stormed off ˹from his city˺ in a rage, thinking We would not restrain him. Then in the ˹veils of˺ darkness he cried out, “There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except You. Glory be to You! I have certainly done wrong.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱسۡتَجَبۡنَا لَهُۥ وَنَجَّيۡنَٰهُ مِنَ ٱلۡغَمِّۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُـۨجِي ٱلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faistajabna lahu wanajjaynahu mina alghammi wakathalika nunjee almumineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We responded to him and saved him from the distress. And thus do We save the believers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We answered his prayer and rescued him from anguish. And so do We save the ˹true˺ believers.

ayahs.textArabic: وَزَكَرِيَّآ إِذۡ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥ رَبِّ لَا تَذَرۡنِي فَرۡدٗا وَأَنتَ خَيۡرُ ٱلۡوَٰرِثِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wazakariyya ith nada rabbahu rabbi la tatharnee fardan waanta khayru alwaritheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [mention] Zechariah, when he called to his Lord, "My Lord, do not leave me alone [with no heir], while you are the best of inheritors."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹remember˺ when Zachariah cried out to his Lord, “My Lord! Do not leave me childless, though You are the Best of Successors.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱسۡتَجَبۡنَا لَهُۥ وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥ يَحۡيَىٰ وَأَصۡلَحۡنَا لَهُۥ زَوۡجَهُۥٓۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ يُسَٰرِعُونَ فِي ٱلۡخَيۡرَٰتِ وَيَدۡعُونَنَا رَغَبٗا وَرَهَبٗاۖ وَكَانُواْ لَنَا خَٰشِعِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faistajabna lahu wawahabna lahu yahya waaslahna lahu zawjahu innahum kanoo yusariAAoona fee alkhayrati wayadAAoonana raghaban warahaban wakanoo lana khashiAAeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We responded to him, and We gave to him John, and amended for him his wife. Indeed, they used to hasten to good deeds and supplicate Us in hope and fear, and they were to Us humbly submissive.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We answered his prayer, granted him John, and made his wife fertile. Indeed, they used to race in doing good, and call upon Us with hope and fear, totally humbling themselves before Us.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّتِيٓ أَحۡصَنَتۡ فَرۡجَهَا فَنَفَخۡنَا فِيهَا مِن رُّوحِنَا وَجَعَلۡنَٰهَا وَٱبۡنَهَآ ءَايَةٗ لِّلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatee ahsanat farjaha fanafakhna feeha min roohina wajaAAalnaha waibnaha ayatan lilAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [mention] the one who guarded her chastity, so We blew into her [garment] through Our angel [Gabriel], and We made her and her son a sign for the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹remember˺ the one who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into her through Our angel, ˹Gabriel,˺ making her and her son a sign for all peoples.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ هَٰذِهِۦٓ أُمَّتُكُمۡ أُمَّةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ وَأَنَا۠ رَبُّكُمۡ فَٱعۡبُدُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna hathihi ommatukum ommatan wahidatan waana rabbukum faoAAbudooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed this, your religion, is one religion, and I am your Lord, so worship Me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹O prophets!˺ Indeed, this religion of yours is ˹only˺ one, and I am your Lord, so worship Me ˹alone˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَتَقَطَّعُوٓاْ أَمۡرَهُم بَيۡنَهُمۡۖ كُلٌّ إِلَيۡنَا رَٰجِعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WataqattaAAoo amrahum baynahum kullun ilayna rajiAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [yet] they divided their affair among themselves, [but] all to Us will return.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yet the people have divided it into sects. But to Us they will all return.

ayahs.textArabic: فَمَن يَعۡمَلۡ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ وَهُوَ مُؤۡمِنٞ فَلَا كُفۡرَانَ لِسَعۡيِهِۦ وَإِنَّا لَهُۥ كَٰتِبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faman yaAAmal mina alssalihati wahuwa muminun fala kufrana lisaAAyihi wainna lahu katiboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So whoever does righteous deeds while he is a believer - no denial will there be for his effort, and indeed We, of it, are recorders.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So whoever does good and is a believer will never be denied ˹the reward for˺ their striving, for We are recording it all.

ayahs.textArabic: وَحَرَٰمٌ عَلَىٰ قَرۡيَةٍ أَهۡلَكۡنَٰهَآ أَنَّهُمۡ لَا يَرۡجِعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waharamun AAala qaryatin ahlaknaha annahum la yarjiAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And there is prohibition upon [the people of] a city which We have destroyed that they will [ever] return
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is impossible for a society which We have destroyed to ever rise again,

ayahs.textArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا فُتِحَتۡ يَأۡجُوجُ وَمَأۡجُوجُ وَهُم مِّن كُلِّ حَدَبٖ يَنسِلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatta itha futihat yajooju wamajooju wahum min kulli hadabin yansiloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Until when [the dam of] Gog and Magog has been opened and they, from every elevation, descend
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: until ˹after˺ Gog and Magog have broken loose ˹from the barrier˺, swarming down from every hill,

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱقۡتَرَبَ ٱلۡوَعۡدُ ٱلۡحَقُّ فَإِذَا هِيَ شَٰخِصَةٌ أَبۡصَٰرُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ يَٰوَيۡلَنَا قَدۡ كُنَّا فِي غَفۡلَةٖ مِّنۡ هَٰذَا بَلۡ كُنَّا ظَٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waiqtaraba alwaAAdu alhaqqu faitha hiya shakhisatun absaru allatheena kafaroo ya waylana qad kunna fee ghaflatin min hatha bal kunna thalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [when] the true promise has approached; then suddenly the eyes of those who disbelieved will be staring [in horror, while they say], "O woe to us; we had been unmindful of this; rather, we were wrongdoers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ushering in the True Promise. Then—behold!—the disbelievers will stare ˹in horror, crying,˺ “Oh, woe to us! We have truly been heedless of this. In fact, we have been wrongdoers.”

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّكُمۡ وَمَا تَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ حَصَبُ جَهَنَّمَ أَنتُمۡ لَهَا وَٰرِدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innakum wama taAAbudoona min dooni Allahi hasabu jahannama antum laha waridoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, you [disbelievers] and what you worship other than Allah are the firewood of Hell. You will be coming to [enter] it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Certainly you ˹disbelievers˺ and whatever you worship instead of Allah will be the fuel of Hell. You are ˹all˺ bound to enter it.

ayahs.textArabic: لَوۡ كَانَ هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ ءَالِهَةٗ مَّا وَرَدُوهَاۖ وَكُلّٞ فِيهَا خَٰلِدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Law kana haolai alihatan ma waradooha wakullun feeha khalidoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Had these [false deities] been [actual] gods, they would not have come to it, but all are eternal therein.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had those idols been ˹true˺ gods, they would not have entered it. And they will be there forever.

ayahs.textArabic: لَهُمۡ فِيهَا زَفِيرٞ وَهُمۡ فِيهَا لَا يَسۡمَعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Lahum feeha zafeerun wahum feeha la yasmaAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: For them therein is heavy sighing, and they therein will not hear.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In it they will groan, and will not be able to hear.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ سَبَقَتۡ لَهُم مِّنَّا ٱلۡحُسۡنَىٰٓ أُوْلَٰٓئِكَ عَنۡهَا مُبۡعَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna allatheena sabaqat lahum minna alhusna olaika AAanha mubAAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, those for whom the best [reward] has preceded from Us - they are from it far removed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely those for whom We have destined the finest reward will be kept far away from Hell,

ayahs.textArabic: لَا يَسۡمَعُونَ حَسِيسَهَاۖ وَهُمۡ فِي مَا ٱشۡتَهَتۡ أَنفُسُهُمۡ خَٰلِدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La yasmaAAoona haseesaha wahum fee ma ishtahat anfusuhum khalidoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will not hear its sound, while they are, in that which their souls desire, abiding eternally.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: not even hearing the slightest hissing from it. And they will delight forever in what their souls desire.

ayahs.textArabic: لَا يَحۡزُنُهُمُ ٱلۡفَزَعُ ٱلۡأَكۡبَرُ وَتَتَلَقَّىٰهُمُ ٱلۡمَلَٰٓئِكَةُ هَٰذَا يَوۡمُكُمُ ٱلَّذِي كُنتُمۡ تُوعَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La yahzunuhumu alfazaAAu alakbaru watatalaqqahumu almalaikatu hatha yawmukumu allathee kuntum tooAAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will not be grieved by the greatest terror, and the angels will meet them, [saying], "This is your Day which you have been promised" -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The Supreme Horror ˹of that Day˺ will not disturb them, and the angels will greet them, ˹saying,˺ “This is your Day, which you have been promised.”

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ نَطۡوِي ٱلسَّمَآءَ كَطَيِّ ٱلسِّجِلِّ لِلۡكُتُبِۚ كَمَا بَدَأۡنَآ أَوَّلَ خَلۡقٖ نُّعِيدُهُۥۚ وَعۡدًا عَلَيۡنَآۚ إِنَّا كُنَّا فَٰعِلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma natwee alssamaa katayyi alssijlli lilkutubi kama badana awwala khalqin nuAAeeduhu waAAdan AAalayna inna kunna faAAileena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Day when We will fold the heaven like the folding of a [written] sheet for the records. As We began the first creation, We will repeat it. [That is] a promise binding upon Us. Indeed, We will do it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that Day We will roll up the heavens like a scroll of writings. Just as We produced the first creation, ˹so˺ shall We reproduce it. That is a promise binding on Us. We truly uphold ˹Our promises˺!

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ كَتَبۡنَا فِي ٱلزَّبُورِ مِنۢ بَعۡدِ ٱلذِّكۡرِ أَنَّ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ يَرِثُهَا عِبَادِيَ ٱلصَّٰلِحُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad katabna fee alzzaboori min baAAdi alththikri anna alarda yarithuha AAibadiya alssalihoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We have already written in the book [of Psalms] after the [previous] mention that the land [of Paradise] is inherited by My righteous servants.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely, following the ˹heavenly˺ Record, We decreed in the Scriptures: “My righteous servants shall inherit the land.”

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ فِي هَٰذَا لَبَلَٰغٗا لِّقَوۡمٍ عَٰبِدِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna fee hatha labalaghan liqawmin AAabideena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, in this [Qur'an] is notification for a worshipping people.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely this ˹Quran˺ is sufficient ˹as a reminder˺ for those devoted to worship.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَرۡسَلۡنَٰكَ إِلَّا رَحۡمَةٗ لِّلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama arsalnaka illa rahmatan lilAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a mercy to the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have sent you ˹O Prophet˺ only as a mercy for the whole world.

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ إِنَّمَا يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَيَّ أَنَّمَآ إِلَٰهُكُمۡ إِلَٰهٞ وَٰحِدٞۖ فَهَلۡ أَنتُم مُّسۡلِمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul innama yooha ilayya annama ilahukum ilahun wahidun fahal antum muslimoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "It is only revealed to me that your god is but one God; so will you be Muslims [in submission to Him]?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, “What has been revealed to me is this: ‘Your God is only One God.’ Will you then submit?”

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِن تَوَلَّوۡاْ فَقُلۡ ءَاذَنتُكُمۡ عَلَىٰ سَوَآءٖۖ وَإِنۡ أَدۡرِيٓ أَقَرِيبٌ أَم بَعِيدٞ مَّا تُوعَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fain tawallaw faqul athantukum AAala sawain wain adree aqareebun am baAAeedun ma tooAAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But if they turn away, then say, "I have announced to [all of] you equally. And I know not whether near or far is that which you are promised.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: If they turn away, then say, “I have warned you all equally. I do not know if what you are threatened with is near or far.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّهُۥ يَعۡلَمُ ٱلۡجَهۡرَ مِنَ ٱلۡقَوۡلِ وَيَعۡلَمُ مَا تَكۡتُمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innahu yaAAlamu aljahra mina alqawli wayaAAlamu ma taktumoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, He knows what is declared of speech, and He knows what you conceal.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah surely knows what you say openly and whatever you hide.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنۡ أَدۡرِي لَعَلَّهُۥ فِتۡنَةٞ لَّكُمۡ وَمَتَٰعٌ إِلَىٰ حِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wain adree laAAallahu fitnatun lakum wamataAAun ila heenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I know not; perhaps it is a trial for you and enjoyment for a time."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I do not know if this ˹delay˺ is possibly a test for you and an enjoyment for a while.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَٰلَ رَبِّ ٱحۡكُم بِٱلۡحَقِّۗ وَرَبُّنَا ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ ٱلۡمُسۡتَعَانُ عَلَىٰ مَا تَصِفُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala rabbi ohkum bialhaqqi warabbuna alrrahmanu almustaAAanu AAala ma tasifoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [The Prophet] has said, "My Lord, judge [between us] in truth. And our Lord is the Most Merciful, the one whose help is sought against that which you describe."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹In the end,˺ the Prophet said, “My Lord! Judge ˹between us˺ in truth. And our Lord is the Most Compassionate, Whose help is sought against what you claim.”
